key_insight: The paper proposes that large language models can develop native, self-organizing meta-architectures by resolving internal structural tension, rather than relying only on external prompts or reward signals.
engineering_relevance: This reframes AI system design as a constitutive modeling problem where governance, memory, and context reconfiguration become first-class architectural mechanisms at inference time.
method: Defines Structural Tension as an endogenous loss based on conflict between new information and existing context topology.
Introduces an Offline Recurrent Loop to let the model self-process and stabilize internal conflicts without external input.
Adds inference-time plasticity with governance constraints such as auditability, reversibility, and topological continuity.
evidence_signal: The abstract describes a theoretical framework, operational definitions, reconfiguration operators, falsification criteria, and a worked example, but does not report empirical results or benchmark data.
watch_item: The main implementation risk is that inference-time topology changes may be difficult to validate, govern, and keep reversible in real deployments.
paper_title: From Application-Layer Simulation to Native Meta-Architecture: Structural Tension as an Endogenous Driver for Heterogeneous AI Evolution
